Subway

Homepage Bus Bus Inside Streetcar Streetcar Inside Subway Subway Inside
As Canada’s first subway and a high-ridership network, it has over 75 stations across four lines, with more planned. Service runs from 6:00 a.m. to 1:30 a.m. Monday to Saturday, and 8:00 a.m. to 1:30 a.m. on Sundays, with shuttle buses operating overnight. The subway connects to major systems like GO Transit, Via Rail, and UP, and links to intercity buses farther out.
When there is track work, shuttle buses. When there's an issue on a train, the line has to stop, people pile onto the platform, and then the huge crowd shoves onto the train, creating an unsafe environment.

- Don’t rush
- Walk down the platform and gauge where the exit will be when you get off, so you don’t have to walk too far
- When it’s busy, and you're going through the gates, go behind people who are going the same way as you.
- When going up escalators, STAY TO THE RIGHT if you're not walking up, and if you are walking up, walk up the left side.
